title: The Crocodile Who Forgot His Own Plan
premise_summary: crocodile forgets his own ambush plan
cover_description: A crocodile frozen mid-"pounce" with his mouth open, staring blankly at a tiny shadow on the ground while a sarcastic girl watches with arms crossed.
script:
[TITLE: The Crocodile Who Forgot His Own Plan]
[AGE: 9-12]
[VOICES: young_sweet, comedic_villain]
[COMEDY_TYPE: villain_fails]

[SETUP]
[SWEET] [DELIVERY: deadpan, unbothered] You’ve been standing on that rock for three hours. [STING: witch_ominous]
[CROC] [DELIVERY: confident, bluster] This is not a rock. [STING: villain_dramatic]
[SWEET] [DELIVERY: deadpan] It’s not moving either. [STING: sweet_pause]

[BEAT_1]
[CROC] [DELIVERY: defensive] I’m conserving energy. [STING: villain_fail]
[SWEET] [DELIVERY: deadpan] For what, a nap? [STING: sweet_eyeroll]

[BEAT_2]
[CROC] [DELIVERY: scrambling] For... the grand ambush! [STING: buildup_short]
[SWEET] [DELIVERY: deadpan] The one where you don’t move? [STING: silence]

[BEAT_3]
[CROC] [DELIVERY: desperate] I was about to pounce! [STING: big_crash]
[SWEET] [DELIVERY: quiet, devastating] You don’t even know what you’re pouncing *at*. [STING: witch_reveal]

[BUTTON]
[CROC] [DELIVERY: deflated, loud] [PUNCHLINE]Fine. What *are* we pouncing at?[/PUNCHLINE] [STING: deflation]
[SWEET] [DELIVERY: deadpan] My shadow. It’s been following me all day. [STING: medium_hit]

[COVER: A crocodile frozen mid-"pounce" with his mouth open, staring blankly at a tiny shadow on the ground while a sarcastic girl watches with arms crossed.]
[PREMISE: crocodile forgets his own ambush plan]
